data.content:
K-33 implementation plan

## File changes
1. internal/web/server.go: Add sync.RWMutex `mu` field. Replace `s.token` reads with `s.currentToken()`. Add `setToken(string)`.
2. internal/web/rotation.go (new): Defines `(*Server).Rotate(stdout io.Writer) error` and `(*Server).RunIdleRotation(ctx, idle, stdout)` and `(*Server).MarkAuthorized()` (resets idle timer). Uses time.Timer; ctx.Done() stops timer.
3. internal/web/cookie.go: hasValidSession compares to currentToken(); on match, MarkAuthorized() resets idle timer.
4. internal/cli/run_web.go: Add --idle-rotate=15m flag. Update banner third line to `Token rotates after <idle-rotate>; SIGUSR1 to rotate sooner; Ctrl+C to stop.`. Wire SIGUSR1 (Unix) via build-tag file. Pass ctx from signal.NotifyContext to run idle loop in goroutine.
5. internal/cli/run_web_signal_unix.go (//go:build !windows): signal.Notify SIGUSR1, rotate on each signal until ctx.Done(). 
6. internal/cli/run_web_signal_windows.go (//go:build windows): no-op.
7. README.md "Web UI" section: paragraph on idle rotation and SIGUSR1.

## Tests
1. internal/web/rotation_test.go (new): TestRotateInvalidatesExistingCookie; TestIdleRotationFiresWithinSmallWindow (50ms); TestMarkAuthorizedResetsTimer; TestRotatePrintsToStdout.
2. internal/cli/run_web_test.go: Update banner test to expect new line text including duration interpolation.

## Decisions
- Test hook: expose (*Server).Rotate(stdout) and (*Server).RunIdleRotation(ctx, idle, stdout) directly on the Server. Tests call them; SIGUSR1 wiring is thin (tested via direct invocation on Unix not signal injection — avoids flake).
- After rotation, cookie value still equals server.token (server replaces the value); old browsers compare old-value to new-token via constant-time compare → 401.
- Goroutine cancel via ctx.Done() and t.Stop().

## Verification
- go build ./...
- go test ./...
- go test -tags integration ./...
- make lint
